Details: Other treatment processes that are applicable for perchlorate reduction are hollow-fiber membrane bioreactor with hydrogen gas supply or permeable barrier with vegetable oil. The anaerobic autotrophic membrane bioreactor and the permeable barrier approach were effective in reducing perchlorate below detection limit of 4 ppb, but concerns remain about the ability of these systems to be scaled up to practical sizes. Electrochemical reduction was also effective, but at temperatures higher than encountered in drinking water treatment.
